{{< pagebreak >}}

# Common spending moments {#common-moments}

The seven days gave you a method. A few everyday moments make spending especially
easy — having time to browse and the mood of the moment,^5^ how much discomfort we
feel when paying,^6^ and, on stressful days, money worry that quietly occupies
attention.^7^ None of these are faults; they are simply moments to meet with a
little more awareness.

```{=typst}
#wcg-cards((
  ("Tiredness", "Late-day choices may feel different after rest."),
  ("Boredom", "A free moment and a phone can quickly become browsing."),
  ("Stress", "A purchase can sometimes feel like short-term relief."),
  ("Celebration", "Positive emotions can also reduce the pause."),
  ("Routine", "The same place and time can invite the same repeated choice."),
), cols: 1)
```

```{=typst}
#block(width: 100%, fill: c-aside, stroke: (left: 3pt + c-gold), radius: (right: 4pt), inset: (x: 16pt, y: 14pt), above: 16pt)[
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 10pt, weight: 700, tracking: 2pt, fill: c-gold)[THE METHOD IS ALWAYS THE SAME]
  #v(6pt)
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 12.5pt, fill: c-body)[
    Notice the moment. Pause for ten quiet seconds. And — if it helps — plan one
    small shift. The same three steps meet every one of these moments.
  ]
]
```

```{=typst}
// ---- Page 21: Everyday automatic spending audit ----
#pagebreak()
```

# Everyday automatic spending audit {#audit}

Some spending happens with no moment of choice at all. This is a gentle once-in-a-
while check — nothing to cancel today, only to see clearly.

```{=typst}
#block(width: 100%, stroke: 0.9pt + c-deep, radius: 5pt, inset: (x: 16pt, y: 14pt), above: 12pt)[
  #wcg-group("1 · Quiet renewals")
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, fill: c-body)[
    Many subscriptions renew unless you cancel,#super[14] and it is easy to keep
    paying for something you rarely use.#super[15]
  ]
  #wcg-field("What renews automatically?", lines: 1)
  #v(2pt)
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, weight: 600, fill: c-deep)[Did I use it as expected?]
  #v(3pt)
  #grid(columns: (auto, auto, auto), column-gutter: 34pt, wcg-check("Yes"), wcg-check("Not sure"), wcg-check("No"))
  #wcg-field("Do I know how to pause or cancel it?", lines: 1)

  #wcg-group("2 · Online convenience")
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, fill: c-body)[
    Online stores are built to be smooth,#super[16] and a familiar app icon can be
    the cue that starts a purchase.#super[3]
  ]
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, weight: 600, fill: c-deep)[Are stored payments or notifications removing my pause?]
  #v(3pt)
  #grid(columns: (auto, auto), column-gutter: 34pt, wcg-check("Yes"), wcg-check("No"))
  #wcg-field("What one small friction could I add?", lines: 1)

  #wcg-group("3 · Spending connected to care")
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, fill: c-body)[
    Spending for people we love can run on autopilot too, wrapped in care and
    routine.#super[2]
  ]
  #wcg-field("Does this purchase still express what I value?", lines: 1)
  #text(font: ("Manrope",), size: 11.5pt, weight: 600, fill: c-deep)[Am I choosing it now, or repeating it automatically?]
  #v(3pt)
  #grid(columns: (auto, auto), column-gutter: 34pt, wcg-check("Choosing now"), wcg-check("Repeating"))
]
```
